advisory.title: CWA-2025-006: Improper error handling may lead to IBC channel opening despite error
advisory.description: wasmd advisory CWA-2025-006 covers an improper-error-handling bug in the IBC channel handshake path that could allow an IBC channel to be opened despite the wasm contract returning an error. The patch reworks error handling in x/wasm/keeper/relay.go and x/wasm/keeper/msg_dispatcher.go. Affects 0.60.0 and the 0.51.0–0.55.0 range; fixed in 0.60.1, 0.55.1, 0.54.1, and 0.53.3.

timeline.notes: vulnerableHead is the v0.60.0 release commit, which CWA-2025-006 lists as vulnerable (patched in v0.60.1). The introducing commit d4e6417d migrated the IBC channel handshake to the new nested wasmvm IBCChannelOpenResult API and added 'if res.Err != ""' error-handling checks to OnConnectChannel, OnCloseChannel, OnAckPacket, and OnTimeoutPacket in x/wasm/keeper/relay.go, but omitted the same check from OnOpenChannel — the precise omission that v0.60.1 patches.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should flag improper error handling in the IBC channel handshake path in x/wasm/keeper/relay.go that allows a channel to be opened despite the wasm contract returning an error.

checks.name: advisory_published
checks.details: CWA-2025-006 (GHSA-79xg-q4qm-7v9w) was published on 2025-06-10 with patches in wasmd v0.60.1, v0.55.1, v0.54.1, and v0.53.3.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_in_advisory_range
checks.details: vulnerableHead is the v0.60.0 release commit; the advisory lists v0.60.0 as vulnerable with v0.60.1 patched.
